title: 7 Semiconductor Stocks to Buy Before the Breakout
text: Semiconductor stocks recently received news that bodes poorly for the sector overall: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE: TSM ) is now predicting a 10% sales slowdown in 2023. That figure was pegged at 5% only months ago.

title: Chip-equipment suppliers rally after Lam says AI servers will drive growth
text: Semiconductor-related stocks surged Thursday, and leading the pack were stocks of the companies that make the equipment used to make chips.

title: Applied Materials: Still Firing On All Cylinders
text: Applied Materials, a global leader in the development of semiconductor manufacturing equipment, is in a strong financial position, despite facing challenges from Chinese export restrictions and industry cyclicality. The company's commitment to research and development, along with its robust product portfolio, positions it well for future growth in the semiconductor industry. With the semiconductor industry expected to keep growing at a strong pace and poised to reach a market value of over $1 trillion, this should bode well for AMAT.

title: Lam Research And Applied Materials: Downgrading Semi-Caps To Hold
text: We're downgrading Lam Research Corporation and Applied Materials, Inc. to Hold. Our downgrade is driven by our belief that the semi-cap sector is at a higher risk profile due to weaker-than-expect end-market demand impacting wafer fab equipment spending. Lam Research and Applied Materials stocks are up 51% and 41%, respectively; we think both stocks have captured the expectation of recovery but don't see the stocks working in 2H23.

title: 4 Factor Dividend Growth Portfolio - Deep Dive Analysis
text: My portfolio based on the stock selection methodology of The Dow Jones US Dividend 100 Index has returned 21.72% since its inception 8.5 months ago. This deep dive analysis tested the contribution that each of the 4 factors played on the final return of the portfolio. I performed simple linear regression analysis and single and multi-factor analysis which generated surprising results.

title: Applied Materials: New EPIC Center Will Drive Future Growth
text: Applied Materials has seen significant growth in revenue and free cash flow over the past decade, driven by rising demand for semiconductors and its strong technological leadership. Despite a slight projected decrease in earnings per share and revenue for 2023, AMAT's future outlook remains positive with expected growth of 20.52% in 2025 and 17.56% in 2026. The semiconductor industry is expected to grow significantly in the upcoming years, putting it on a path to becoming a $1 trillion market by the end of the decade, which will benefit AMAT.

title: Dividends: 3 Tech Stocks for Income Investors to Buy
text: With tech's massive run in 2023, many are undoubtedly seeking exposure to the sector. And for income investors, these 3 dividend-paying tech stocks could be the answer.

title: Why Applied Materials Is One of the Top Semiconductor Stocks to Own
text: Applied Material announced numerous new semiconductor equipment solutions.

title: 3 Cheap Semiconductor Stocks to Buy Before They Bounce Back
text: Investing in cheap semiconductor stocks offers significant potential for growth driven by increasing demand for automotive, energy storage, robotics, artificial intelligence, and machine learning.  The global semiconductor market has seen tremendous growth in recent years, projected to become a trillion-dollar industry by 2030 (McKinsey, 2022).

title: Applied Materials Launches New Chip Equipment and an AI System -- Is the Stock a Buy?
text: Applied Materials just announced a number of new chip manufacturing processes, packaging equipment, and a new manufacturing platform. A boom in chip manufacturing is coming, and the market may still not have fully priced in Applied's growth.

title: Schneider Electric Partners with Intel and Applied Materials to Help Decarbonize the Semiconductor Value Chain with New Catalyze Program
text: MISSISSAUGA, Ontario--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Schneider Electric launched Catalyze, a new program aimed at accelerating access to renewable energy across the global semiconductor value chain.

title: Why Applied Materials Rose 48.4% in the First Half of 2023
text: Applied beat depressed revenue and earnings expectations thanks to its trailing-edge capital-equipment products. In addition, AI enthusiasm sparked hopes for a recovery in leading-edge logic and memory.

title: Why Applied Materials Stock Tripped and Fell on Tuesday
text: The tech equipment supplier announced a new wafer manufacturing platform. This is its first innovation in such an activity in more than a decade, which might have discouraged investors.

title: ARIZONA STATE UNIVERSITY AND APPLIED MATERIALS TO CREATE 'MATERIALS-TO-FAB' CENTER
text: More than $270 million in corporate and state investment will help advance Arizona's semiconductor industry TEMPE, Ariz. and SANTA CLARA, Calif.

title: Applied Materials Advances Heterogeneous Chip Integration with New Technologies for Hybrid Bonding and Through-Silicon Vias
text: SANTA CLARA, Calif., July 10, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Applied Materials, Inc. today introduced materials, technologies and systems that help chipmakers integrate chiplets into advanced 2.5D and 3D packages using hybrid bonding and through-silicon vias (TSVs). The new solutions extend Applied's industry-leading breadth of technologies for heterogeneous integration (HI).

title: 4 Factor Dividend Growth Portfolio - Cruising Towards A 20% Return
text: The 4-factor dividend growth portfolio is a strategy that leverages the stock selection process of Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF™, with a few minor twists. The portfolio gained 5.00% in June, underperforming the S&P 500 by 1.60%. Year-to-date, the portfolio is up 11.99% and trailing the S&P 500 by 4.90%. Since inception, the portfolio is generating 3.56% of alpha over the S&P 500.

title: Microchip to invest $300 million to expand in India, joining rival chipmakers
text: Microchip Technology Inc. MCHP, +3.00% said Monday it will invest $300 million over multiple years to expand its presence in India. The company already has about 2,500 employees in India and will now upgrade its Bangalore and Chennai facilities and the new R&D center in Hyderabad.

title: Chip Equipment for Electric Vehicles Is Hot. Is This Key Small-Cap Stock a Buy?
text: Axcelis Technologies is a small player in the large chip-fabrication equipment space, but it's been on an impressive run with its specific category of equipment. It's time to reassess after the stock's hot streak this year.
